latest.title: AGM Trading Update
latest.content:
15 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1874I
Mission Group PLC (The)
15 June 2026
15 June 2026
The Mission Group plc
("MISSION", the "Company" or "Group")
AGM Trading Update
Trading in line with expectations, strong Client retention and wins, continued focus on strategic delivery
Ahead of its Annual General Meeting held today,
MISSION
Group plc (AIM:TMG), a collective of sector-leading Creative, Sports and MarTech Agencies, provides the following update on trading for the period 1 January to 15 June 2026.
Trading in the current financial year remains in line with the Board's expectations. Against an ongoing backdrop of wider macroeconomic uncertainty this has been driven by continued strong Client retention, new Client wins and the benefits of the strategic actions completed earlier in the year to simplify and strengthen the Group's operating platform.
New Client wins were achieved across the Group's five practice areas: Integrated Marketing, Sports & Events, Property, PR and Healthcare. These included Westminster Council, Puma, Amaala Yacht Club, PwC, the International Tennis Federation and Volleyball World.
Good progress continues to be made against the key strategic priorities for 2026 outlined in the Group's full year results in March: US expansion execution, agency integration, consultancy offer launch, growth engine activation, capital allocation discipline and redefining the
MISSION
proposition. This includes further expansion of the pipeline of Client opportunities in the US within the Group's Mongoose Sports & Events practice. In addition, to further strengthen the combined capabilities of the Group's Integrated Marketing practice, Bray Leino was pleased to welcome globally recognised creative leader Wayne Deakin into the role of Chief Creative Officer.
A further update on trading will be provided, as usual, in a half year update in July.
ENDS
John Carey, Chief Executive
Giles Lee, Chief Financial Officer
Via Houston
The
MISSION
Group PLC
Simon Bridges/Andrew Potts/Harry Rees
Canaccord Genuity Limited
(Financial Adviser, Nominated Adviser and Broker)
020 7523 8000
Peter Tracey
Blackdown Partners Limited
(Financial Adviser)
020 3807 8484
Kate Hoare/Charlie Barker
Houston
(PR advisers)
077 3303 2695 / 0204 529 0549
E:
mission@houston.co.uk
NOTES TO EDITORS
MISSION Group is a collective of specialist agencies delivering marketing, communications and digital services for Clients across the UK and internationally. The Group combines deep sector expertise with connected capabilities across brand, digital, customer engagement, sponsorship, media, social, technology and communications.
Find out more at
www.themission.co.uk
Change of Accounting Reference Date
As previously announced, as a result of the change in the Group's accounting reference date, the Group's financial year will now end on 30 September annually, with its reporting timetable as follows:
· publication of audited annual financial statements for the 12-month period to 31 December 2025, by no later than 30 June 2026 (published on 4 June 2026);
· publication of unaudited interim accounts for the 6-month period to 30 June 2026, by no later than 30 September 2026;
· publication of audited annual financial statements for the 9-month period to 30 September 2026, by no later than 30 March 2027;
· publication of unaudited interim accounts for the 6-month period to 31 March 2027, by no later than 30 June 2027; and
· publication of audited annual financial statements for the 12-month period to 30 September 2027, by no later than 31 March 2028.
From then, annual and interim results will be published each year for the 12 months to 30 September and 6 months to 31 March, respectively.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.content:

RNS Number : 8945G
Mission Group PLC (The)
04 June 2026
4 June 2026
THE MISSION GROUP plc
("MISSION", "the Group")
Notice of AGM and Posting of Annual Report
The
MISSION
Group plc (AIM: TMG),
the Brand Performance Group, comprising of digital marketing and specialist communications Agencies
,
announces that copies of the Annual Report and Accounts in respect of the year ended 31 December 2025 have been sent to shareholders.
The Company also announces that Notice of the Annual General Meeting and the associated Proxy Form has been sent to shareholders. The Company's Annual General Meeting will be held on Monday 15 June 2026 at 12 noon at the offices of the Company, Fourth Floor, The Manufactory, 1-8 Alfred Mews, London W1T 7AA.
The Annual Report and the notice of AGM are available now on the Company's website at

RNS Number : 3089F
Mission Group PLC (The)
21 May 2026
21 May 2026
The Mission Group plc
("MISSION" the "Company" or the "Group")
DIRECTOR/PDMR SHAREHOLDINGS
The
MISSION
Group plc (AIM: TMG) the Brand Performance Group, comprising of digital marketing and specialist communications Agencies,
has been notified of the following dealings in the Company's ordinary shares of 10p each ("
Ordinary Shares
"):
·
On 19 May 2026, David Zwirn, a person closely associated with Claudine Collins, Non-Executive Director, purchased 144,000 Ordinary Shares at a price of 13.99p per Ordinary Share.
·
On 20 May 2026, Jon Kempster, Non-Executive Director, purchased 68,852 Ordinary Shares at a price of 15.26p per Ordinary Share. His beneficial holding is 68,852 Ordinary Shares.
·
On 21 May 2026, Claudine Collins, Non-Executive Director, purchased 87,793 Ordinary Shares at a price of 17p per Ordinary Share. Her beneficial holding including those Ordinary Shares held by persons closely associated with her is 231,793 Ordinary Shares; and
·
On 21 May 2026, John Carey, Chief Executive Officer, purchased 175,587 Ordinary Shares at a price of 17p per Ordinary Share. His beneficial holding is 200,587 Ordinary Shares.

company_30s.what_they_do: TMG Group plc is a UK‑based business that delivers integrated marketing and communication solutions for a range of clients, from consumer brands to financial services. The company offers digital marketing, media planning, data analytics, and creative production, helping brands reach customers across TV, online, social, and emerging platforms.
company_30s.why_they_matter: In a market where digital engagement is becoming a core competitive edge, TMG’s end‑to‑end service model positions it to capture growing demand for data‑driven, omni‑channel marketing. Its clients are mid‑ to large‑cap firms that need sophisticated media buying and analytics to stay ahead of fast‑moving consumer trends.
company_30s.current_state: TMG is in a rebuilding phase. Recent board changes and a new CEO signal a pivot toward tighter cost control and a focus on higher‑margin services. The company has been reducing its share count through a buy‑back program, which suggests confidence in its balance sheet. The share is trading well below the valuation it enjoyed a few years ago, indicating that investors have largely priced in a prolonged recovery.

rns_narrative: Leadership overhaul – a new CEO and board reshuffle aim to streamline decision‑making and sharpen strategic focus.
Capital management – a share buy‑back and director share‑holding adjustments show that insiders are betting on the business’s future.
Financial transparency – regular AGM updates, interim results and investor presentations keep the market informed of performance trends and future plans.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bullish optimism fueled by leadership change and a share buy‑back
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls highlight the new CEO’s track record, the buy‑back as evidence of undervaluation, and the growth share scheme as a way to attract talent.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the company’s weak earnings history, the risk of dilution from the share scheme, and the uncertainty around whether the new strategy will translate into sustainable revenue growth.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from cautious to moderately optimistic following the CEO appointment and the announcement of a share buy‑back, but remains fragile if earnings fail to improve.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends data for the ticker is flat, meaning there is no current public buzz – a classic contrarian signal that a surprise is likely when a catalyst arrives.

near_term_catalysts: The next investor presentation could reveal a new high‑margin product line or a strategic partnership that boosts top‑line growth.
Completion of the share buy‑back at a price that reflects intrinsic value would signal management confidence and could lift the share price.
A positive earnings surprise in the upcoming quarterly report would validate the new operating model.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike at the current support level, a clear uptick in earnings guidance, and a tightening of the price range that indicates a breakout. A sustained rally above the 50‑day moving average would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early stage of a recovery. The APEX score suggests a tight compression around the support zone, which historically precedes a breakout. If the price holds above this zone and volume confirms interest, the next phase of the cycle is likely underway.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company has crashed more than 70 % from its peak, creating a steep upside if it recovers to the midpoint of that range. With a new CEO steering cost control and a buy‑back that shows insider confidence, the probability of a rebound is high. Historically, similar deep‑crash setups have returned 150‑200 % once the market re‑recognises value.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If earnings continue to miss guidance or the growth share scheme dilutes the equity base, the stock could stay depressed. A failure to secure new high‑margin revenue streams would leave the company in a prolonged low‑margin cycle.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 150‑200 % if the share climbs to the midpoint of the pre‑crash range. Potential downside: 30‑40 % if the share remains near its current low. The asymmetry is strongly in favour of a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed breakout above the current support zone, ideally with a volume surge of at least 20 % above average. Allocate 5‑7 % of discretionary capital, set a stop 10 % below the breakout level, and trail the stop as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the company delivers a positive earnings surprise or announces a new strategic partnership. A clear upward revision in revenue guidance or a new client contract worth >£10 m would be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: Only consider exiting if the company announces bankruptcy, delisting, or a material fraud issue. Otherwise, the setup remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a deep crash, a fresh leadership team and a share buy‑back in place, TMG is primed for a rebound; the upside is large, the downside is capped, and the timing of a mean‑reversion breakout is likely in the next few months.
